title: Roman empire
description: Test your knowledge on the Roman empire!
topic_path: From Roman Republic to Roman Empire

assessment_items.question_content:
An autocratic government is a system in which decisions are made by

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: There is not a body of elected representatives in an autocracy.

assessment_items.question_content:
When did the Roman Republic end and the Roman Empire begin, according to most historians?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Roman Empire didn't end with Augustus.

choices.content: In 27 BCE, when Augustus became the sole ruler of Rome
In 44 BCE, when Julius Caesar was assassinated

assessment_items.question_content:
Why do historians make the distinction between the Roman Republic and the Roman Empire?


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The distinction between the Roman Republic and the Roman Empire is a political one.

choices.content: It was a transition from a system of elected representatives and limited power to a system in which one man held most political power
Life for ordinary Romans changed drastically with the beginning of the empire
There is no meaningful difference; it is convenient to make the split at Augustus because he was the ruler when the calendar switched from BCE to CE
